qas.a: Career Field Education and Training Plan that identifies life-cycle education, training requirements, training support resources, and minimum core tasks for this specialty.
Supervisors plan, manage, and control training within a specialty using both parts of the plan.

-Abbreviations and terms
-Information on specific career progression
-Duties and responsibilities
-Training Strategies
-Career paths

sorting facility for inbound/outbound mail prior for onward movement to mili. base or mail control activity.
recruits, trains, educates AF personnel.
directory for all AF enlisted classifications, codes, and identifiers.
A comprehensive task that describes job or duty position. Supervisors use to doc task qualification.
unit that supports AF personnel and training through analyses, promotion tests, and study guides.
DoD operated to serve military overseas where US postal service does not operate; postage stamps, money orders, parcel mailing, and parcel delivery.
Course designed for use at unit to qualify or aid qualifications in duty position, programs, or on piece of equipment.

Automates management, enrollment, transcription of upgrade training records. Reduces paper, print, shipping costs.

CC or unit level responsibility for unit-appointed positions and associated tasks.

ensures completion of all work requirements; includes a Master Task Listing
